clusters.neutral_headline: Rescuers free four more men from flooded Laos cave, two still missing
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera, Guardian World and others

items.title: 5/30: Saturday Morning
items.standfirst: Four more miners in Laos were freed from the cave they were trapped in for 10 days, while 2 remain missing, rescuers say. Meanwhile, uncertainty remains over an Iran ceasefire.
items.content: Four more miners in Laos were freed from the cave they were trapped in for 10 days, while 2 remain missing, rescuers say. Meanwhile, uncertainty remains over an Iran ceasefire.

items.title: Rescuers free four more men from flooded Laos cave, two still missing
items.standfirst: Five of seven men who entered cave seeking gold are now out after being trapped for 10 days.
items.content: Five of seven men who entered cave seeking gold are now out after being trapped for 10 days.

items.title: 4 more Laos miners freed from cave after being trapped for 10 days, rescuers say
items.standfirst: Four more Laos miners who were trapped in a cave for 10 days were freed, rescuers say, while two remain missing.
items.content: Four more Laos miners who were trapped in a cave for 10 days were freed, rescuers say, while two remain missing.

items.title: Four more men freed from flooded Laos cave in hazardous rescue mission
items.standfirst: Two still missing as divers make their way deeper into cave through muddy water and sharp rocks to find themFour more miners who were trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for 10 days have been freed by divers, but two people are still missing as rescuers continue to crawl through narrow, deluged tunnels and sharp rocks to find them.The first of the party of seven men was rescued on Friday in a perilous rescue mission which has required teams to drain water from the cave and navigate collapse hazards. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.content: Two of the seven villagers who entered the narrow tunnels in search for gold on 20 May are still missing.

items.title: Four more men rescued from flooded cave after becoming trapped while searching for gold

items.content: Five men have now been freed after spending more than a week trapped in a flooded cave in Laos, according to rescuers, with two others still missing

items.title: Four people trapped in flooded cave in Laos pulled to safety
items.standfirst: Four people trapped for 10 days in a flooded cave in Laos have been safely evacuated by rescuers.
items.content: Four people trapped for 10 days in a flooded cave in Laos have been safely evacuated by rescuers.

items.title: Video shows first of five men trapped in Laos cave rescued
items.standfirst: The men from Laos have been trapped underground for over a week after entering a cave to look for gold. A team of rescuers is working to bring out all of the five so far located, although two others are still missing.
items.content: The men from Laos have been trapped underground for over a week after entering a cave to look for gold. A team of rescuers is working to bring out all of the five so far located, although two others are still missing.

items.title: Four still remain trapped in the flooded Laos cave after one person was rescued
items.standfirst: Rescuers have begun removing miners who have been trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for more than a week. One person was rescued while four others remain trapped. NBC News' Janis Mackey Frayer reports on the rescue operation underway.
items.content: Rescuers have begun removing miners who have been trapped in a flooded cave in Laos for more than a week. One person was rescued while four others remain trapped. NBC News' Janis Mackey Frayer reports on the rescue operation underway.

items.title: Man rescued after more than a week trapped in flooded cave in Laos

clusters.title: Trump decision on Iran deal yet to come as Hegseth talks negotiations
clusters.neutral_headline: No deal announced after Trump meeting to make 'final determination' on Iran
bullet_summary: The US president met his advisers, after officials confirmed the US and Iran had agreed a framework of a deal
Reported by CBS News, BBC World, NBC World and others

items.title: Trump decision on Iran deal yet to come as Hegseth talks negotiations
items.standfirst: President Trump held a meeting with key advisers on Friday to hammer out a "final determination" on a potential deal with Iran, but no word has come on a decision.
items.content: President Trump held a meeting with key advisers on Friday to hammer out a "final determination" on a potential deal with Iran, but no word has come on a decision.

items.title: Uncertainty remains over Iran ceasefire extension after Trump's marathon White House meeting
items.standfirst: President Trump signaled Friday before a marathon meeting at the White House that he would make a final decision on the war with Iran, but uncertainty remains over where things stand after he failed to announce a deal or ceasefire extension after the meeting.
items.content: President Trump signaled Friday before a marathon meeting at the White House that he would make a final decision on the war with Iran, but uncertainty remains over where things stand after he failed to announce a deal or ceasefire extension after the meeting.

items.title: No deal announced after Trump meeting to make 'final determination' on Iran
items.standfirst: The US president met his advisers, after officials confirmed the US and Iran had agreed a framework of a deal.
items.content: The US president met his advisers, after officials confirmed the US and Iran had agreed a framework of a deal.

items.title: Trump is stalling deal talks with ‘excessive demands,’ Iran official says
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump has yet to make a decision on a deal that could extend the current ceasefire with Iran, with no announcement on Friday following a Situation Room meeting where he said he would make a “final determination.”Oil prices have dipped this week amid persistent reports that Trump and Iran could be close to sealing an agreement that has eluded the two sides for over a month
items.content: President Donald Trump has yet to make a decision on a deal that could extend the current ceasefire with Iran, with no announcement on Friday following a Situation Room meeting where he said he would make a “final determination.”Oil prices have dipped this week amid persistent reports that Trump and Iran could be close to sealing an agreement that has eluded the two sides for over a month

items.title: US ‘more than capable’ of resuming war against Iran, Pete Hegseth says
items.standfirst: Pentagon chief also tells Singapore defence summit of ‘alarm’ at China’s military buildup but says US does not seek ‘needless confrontation’The US warned on Saturday it was “more than capable” of resuming war with Iran after President Donald Trump said any peace deal must adhere to his red lines, including Tehran never being able to develop nuclear weapons.The White House had signalled Trump was close to a decision on an initial deal on Friday after weeks of mixed signals in tenuous negotiations, though Tehran denied there was a final agreement on ending the Middle East conflict that has jolted the global economy. Continue reading...
items.content: Pentagon chief also tells Singapore defence summit of ‘alarm’ at China’s military buildup but says US does not seek ‘needless confrontation’The US warned on Saturday it was “more than capable” of resuming war with Iran after President Donald Trump said any peace deal must adhere to his red lines, including Tehran never being able to develop nuclear weapons.The White House had signalled Trump was close to a decision on an initial deal on Friday after weeks of mixed signals in tenuous negotiations, though Tehran denied there was a final agreement on ending the Middle East conflict that has jolted the global economy. Continue reading...

items.title: Trump claims to be on verge of peace deal but Iran signals no agreement reached
items.standfirst: US president did not announce decision on deal that could open strait of Hormuz after two-hour situation room meetDonald Trump has claimed he could approve an Iran peace deal on Friday that contains major concessions from Tehran, including the opening of the strait of Hormuz and the elimination of the country’s nuclear programme. However, top Iranian officials signalled a final agreement had not been reached.The two versions indicate Trump may once again be practising his “art of the deal” as he seeks to talk his way out of a war that has disrupted global energy supplies and rocked the world economy. Continue reading...
items.content: US president did not announce decision on deal that could open strait of Hormuz after two-hour situation room meetDonald Trump has claimed he could approve an Iran peace deal on Friday that contains major concessions from Tehran, including the opening of the strait of Hormuz and the elimination of the country’s nuclear programme. However, top Iranian officials signalled a final agreement had not been reached.The two versions indicate Trump may once again be practising his “art of the deal” as he seeks to talk his way out of a war that has disrupted global energy supplies and rocked the world economy. Continue reading...

clusters.title: WHO chief visits eastern DR Congo amid Ebola outbreak
clusters.neutral_headline: WHO chief visits Bunia, epicentre of the Ebola outbreak
bullet_summary: The health organization said latest official figures showed 906 suspected cases and 223 suspected deaths
Anaïs Legand, from the WHO’s high threat pathogens team, said the revised death rate estimate is based on confirmed cases

items.title: WHO chief visits eastern DR Congo amid Ebola outbreak
items.standfirst: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus traveled to Bunia, in DR Congo on Saturday to show his support to the residents of the capital of Ituri Province, the epicentre of a severe Ebola virus outbreak. There have been at least 1,077 suspected cases in the country since the outbreak was declared on May 15.
items.content: The head of the World Health Organization (WHO) Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus traveled to Bunia, in DR Congo on Saturday to show his support to the residents of the capital of Ituri Province, the epicentre of a severe Ebola virus outbreak. There have been at least 1,077 suspected cases in the country since the outbreak was declared on May 15.

items.title: WHO chief visits Congo as Ebola cases spread faster than response
items.standfirst: The health organization said latest official figures showed 906 suspected cases and 223 suspected deaths.
items.content: The health organization said latest official figures showed 906 suspected cases and 223 suspected deaths.

items.title: WHO chief arrives in Congo as officials fear Ebola outbreak spread
items.standfirst: World Health Organization Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus arrived in Congo as health officials continue to battle a severe Ebola outbreak and misinformation.
items.content: World Health Organization Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus arrived in Congo as health officials continue to battle a severe Ebola outbreak and misinformation.

items.title: WHO chief visits Bunia, epicentre of the Ebola outbreak
items.standfirst: WHO Chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus is visiting Bunia in the DRC, the epicentre of the Ebola outbreak.
items.content: WHO Chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus is visiting Bunia in the DRC, the epicentre of the Ebola outbreak.

items.title: WHO puts Ebola outbreak death rate at ‘huge’ 30-50% as chief arrives in DRC
items.standfirst: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus calls for ceasefire among armed groups to help avoid deaths from preventable diseaseThe death rate of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is between 30% and 50%, the World Health Organization has said, as its head arrived in the country to support efforts to contain the disease.Anaïs Legand, from the WHO’s high threat pathogens team, said the revised death rate estimate is based on confirmed cases. “It’s huge. It means that up to five out of 10 people are likely to die,” Legand told reporters in Geneva. Continue reading...
items.content: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus calls for ceasefire among armed groups to help avoid deaths from preventable diseaseThe death rate of the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is between 30% and 50%, the World Health Organization has said, as its head arrived in the country to support efforts to contain the disease.Anaïs Legand, from the WHO’s high threat pathogens team, said the revised death rate estimate is based on confirmed cases. “It’s huge. It means that up to five out of 10 people are likely to die,” Legand told reporters in Geneva. Continue reading...

items.title: What’s Behind the Blue Origin Rocket Explosion?
items.standfirst: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket exploded in a massive fireball while undergoing a test on a launchpad Thursday. Peter Elstrom explains what that means for the Bezos-backed company and its race with SpaceX. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.standfirst: French club’s triumph in penalty shootout over London side in win for Gulf sovereign wealth over US capital
items.content: French club’s triumph in penalty shootout over London side in win for Gulf sovereign wealth over US capital

items.title: PSG retain Champions League after penalty shootout win over Arsenal
items.standfirst: Arsenal lose a penalty shootout as Paris St-Germain win the Champions League for a second successive year.
items.content: Arsenal lose a penalty shootout as Paris St-Germain win the Champions League for a second successive year.

items.title: PSG join Europe's elite with back-to-back titles
items.standfirst: Paris St-Germain underline their status as one of European football's greatest ever teams by retaining their Champions League crown with victory against Arsenal.
items.content: Paris St-Germain underline their status as one of European football's greatest ever teams by retaining their Champions League crown with victory against Arsenal.

items.title: PSG beat Arsenal on penalties to win back-to-back Champions League titles
items.standfirst: Defending champions Paris Saint-Germain beat Arsenal 4-3 on penalties to win their second consecutive Champions League title after a 1-1 draw in a tense final in Budapest on Saturday, cementing their status among the giants of European football.
items.content: Defending champions Paris Saint-Germain beat Arsenal 4-3 on penalties to win their second consecutive Champions League title after a 1-1 draw in a tense final in Budapest on Saturday, cementing their status among the giants of European football.

items.title: PSG and Arsenal chase European glory at UEFA Champions League final
items.standfirst: Arsenal and Paris Saint Germain will face off in a thrilling final under the lights of the Pushkas Arena in Budapest tonight. PSG has high hopes of winning the prestigious title for the second year in a row, whilst the Gunners are hoping to lift the trophy for the first time. Get a feel of the electric atmosphere inside the arena with FRANCE 24 journalist James Vasina.
items.content: Arsenal and Paris Saint Germain will face off in a thrilling final under the lights of the Pushkas Arena in Budapest tonight. PSG has high hopes of winning the prestigious title for the second year in a row, whilst the Gunners are hoping to lift the trophy for the first time. Get a feel of the electric atmosphere inside the arena with FRANCE 24 journalist James Vasina.

items.title: Live: PSG hold on to Champions League title after dramatic shootout win over Arsenal
items.standfirst: Paris Saint-Germain won a second consecutive Champions League title on Saturday after edging Arsenal in a tense final that went all the way to a penalty shootout. Kai Havertz had fired Arsenal into a sixth-minute lead, but Ousmane Dembélé equalised for the title holders with a penalty. Follow the match on our liveblog.
items.content: Paris Saint-Germain won a second consecutive Champions League title on Saturday after edging Arsenal in a tense final that went all the way to a penalty shootout. Kai Havertz had fired Arsenal into a sixth-minute lead, but Ousmane Dembélé equalised for the title holders with a penalty. Follow the match on our liveblog.

items.title: Festive atmosphere in Budapest as PSG and Arsenal eye Champions League title
items.standfirst: It's the pinnacle of football. Kick off at the Champions League final in Budapest is just a few hours away. Arsenal are trying to win it for the first time, while Paris Saint-Germain wants to become only the second team to win it twice in a row. The atmosphere in Budapest is festive to say the least, details from the ground with FRANCE 24 journalist Selina Sykes and freelance sports journalist Karim Baldé.
items.content: It's the pinnacle of football. Kick off at the Champions League final in Budapest is just a few hours away. Arsenal are trying to win it for the first time, while Paris Saint-Germain wants to become only the second team to win it twice in a row. The atmosphere in Budapest is festive to say the least, details from the ground with FRANCE 24 journalist Selina Sykes and freelance sports journalist Karim Baldé.

items.title: PSG and Arsenal supporters arrive in Budapest for Champions League final
items.standfirst: The Champions league final is set for tonight. Arsenal and PSG will face off under the lights of the Pushkas Arena in Budapest. PSG has high hopes of winning the prestigious title for the second year in a row… a feat that would be nothing less than legendary. Story by Caroline Baum.
items.content: The Champions league final is set for tonight. Arsenal and PSG will face off under the lights of the Pushkas Arena in Budapest. PSG has high hopes of winning the prestigious title for the second year in a row… a feat that would be nothing less than legendary. Story by Caroline Baum.

clusters.title: Kanye West concert in Italy cancelled over ‘public order and safety issues’
clusters.neutral_headline: Kanye West concert in Italy cancelled over ‘public order and safety issues’
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Kanye West concert in Italy cancelled over ‘public order and safety issues’
items.standfirst: Reggio Emilia prefect stops gig after ‘concerns’ from local Jewish community over rapper’s previous antisemitic remarksA Kanye West concert in Italy has been cancelled over “public order and safety issues”.The 48-year-old rapper, who changed his name to Ye in 2021, was due to perform at the Pulse of Gaia festival at the RCF Arena in Reggio Emilia on 18 July, but the city’s prefect, Salvatore Angieri, stopped the gigs after “concerns” from the local Jewish community over previous antisemitic remarks by West. Continue reading...

Continue reading...

items.title: Truck carrying returning refugees crashes in eastern Afghanistan, killing 22

items.content: KABUL, Afghanistan — A truck carrying Afghan refugees returning from neighboring Pakistan overturned on a highway in eastern Afghanistan on Saturday, killing at least 22 people and injuring about 36 others, most of them women and children, officials said

items.title: Eighteen people killed in Afghanistan truck crash, including 10 children
items.standfirst: Truck was carrying Afghan families returning Pakistan when it overturned, official saysA truck overturned in eastern Afghanistan on Saturday, killing 18 people on board including 10 children, a provincial official told Agence France-Presse.Deadly traffic crashes are common in Afghanistan, due in part to poor roads after decades of conflict, dangerous driving and a lack of regulation.
